checks.a_analysis: The output content closely follows the source document's text. No evidence of hallucinated paragraphs or fabricated sections was found.
No fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs were detected. One existing URL in the source was omitted (see C5), but nothing new was invented.
All numbered primary sections (1 through 9) and all pages are represented in the output document. While some sub-content is missing, no entire pages or top-level sections were dropped.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output contains several significant omissions. Specifically, it omits two subsection headings on Page 1 ('a. 911/NG911 in the Media' and 'b. AddressNC and Census Geospatial Data'). It also omits a critical source URL on Page 3 and three distinct sub-bullet questions regarding source data on Page 5. These omissions represent substantial losses of information and structural markers.

1. 'a. 911/NG911 in the Media' and 'b. AddressNC and Census Geospatial Data' (Page 1 subsection headings)
2. '(http://www.ncgicc.org/Portals/3/documents/Location_of_County_GIS_Operations.pdf)' (Page 3 URL)
3. 'o Is current satellite imagery... o Would color infrared imagery... o Would National Agriculture Imagery Program...' (Page 5 sub-bullets)
checks.b_evidence_output:

2. 'A survey in 2010 found a variety of departmental locations for GIS' (reading_order 19 ends here)
3. 'Source data questions:' (reading_order 43 ends here with no nested list content)

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document and found no instances where text blocks or paragraphs have been duplicated inappropriately.

The heading hierarchy is significantly broken and inconsistent. Major sections (1-9) are treated inconsistently: items 1-3 are in an unordered list, item 4 is H2, items 5-6 are H3, item 7 is H2, and items 8-9 are H3. Additionally, section headings '3.a. 911/NG911 in the Media' and '3.b. AddressNC and Census Geospatial Data' from page 1 are entirely missing from the output, which disrupts document navigation.
checks.b_evidence_source: a. 911/NG911 in the Media (Page 1); 5. GICC Website (Page 4); 8. Other Items (Page 6)
checks.b_evidence_output: [Headings missing for 3a/3b]; { "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "5. GICC Website" }; { "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "8. Other Items" }

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables.
The text content generally follows the logical reading order of the source document, despite the structural issues with headings.

There are two issues with list conversion. First, the numbered items 1, 2, and 3 on page 1 were converted to an unordered list and lost their '1.', '2.', and '3.' numbering labels in the item text. Second, a nested list on page 5 under 'Source data questions:' containing three sub-bullet points (circles in the source) is completely omitted from the JSON output.
checks.b_evidence_source: o Is current satellite imagery (applied in 1996) suitable as the source for classification? o Would color infrared imagery... o Would National Agriculture Imagery Program (NAIP) leaf-on imagery...
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in reading_order 43 ends at 'Source data questions:' without including the nested sub-items.

checks.a_analysis: A URL for a PDF document is present in the text on page 3 but was not converted into a functional, clickable link in the output.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "A survey in 2010 found a variety of departmental locations for GIS (http://www.ncgicc.org/Portals/3/documents/Location_of_County_GIS_Operations.pdf)."

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully represents the single-column text layout of the source PDF. The organization of information, including the hierarchical numbering of sections and subsections, matches the original document. Headers are appropriately styled to provide visual structure consistent with the source. The addition of a Table of Contents sidebar is an expected navigation feature.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present.
The source PDF is a text-based document without any meaningful images, logos, or diagrams. No missing or broken images were identified in the HTML output.

A thorough review of the HTML output confirms that no body content (paragraphs, list items) has been duplicated. The appearance of headings in the Table of Contents and the body text is an intentional accessibility feature and not a content defect. The numbering error present in the source document (two sections labeled 'b' under item 3) is correctly maintained in the output.
